findings.description: The exception tuple in run_polling_threadpool (OSError, httpx.HTTPError, RuntimeError, ValueError, TypeError, KeyError) is manually constructed and partially overlaps with PAPERLESS_CALL_EXCEPTIONS but adds RuntimeError and TypeError. This parallel definition could drift; a single canonical tuple or a composed one (PAPERLESS_CALL_EXCEPTIONS + (RuntimeError, TypeError)) would make the relationship explicit.

OcrProcessor names its provider field 'ocr_provider' while ClassificationProcessor names its provider field 'classifier'. The asymmetry (domain-prefixed vs role-named) is a minor naming drift: 'ocr_provider' vs 'classifier' rather than a consistent pattern like 'provider' or '{domain}_provider' in both.

get_or_create_tag_ids duplicates the match-then-create-then-refresh-on-failure pattern from _get_or_create_item_id rather than delegating to it. The tag path handles matching_algorithm inference and batching, but the core retry-with-refresh logic is repeated inline (~20 lines). This is not trivial to unify due to the batching, but is worth noting as structural duplication.

update_document (content + tags) and update_document_metadata (arbitrary fields) are two overlapping PATCH endpoints to the same URL. update_document could be expressed as update_document_metadata(doc_id, content=content, tags=tags) if 'content' were added to _METADATA_FIELDS, eliminating a separate method with its own raise_for_status/logging.
